Champion wrestler Saleh Mohammadi, 19, was reportedly killed in a public hanging along with Mehdi Ghasemi and Saeed Davoudi in the city of Qom on Thursday. Mohammadi was sentenced to death in February, less than three weeks after his arrest, over the murder of a security agent during the anti-regime protests on January 8, according to Amnesty International. Ellis Matthews, 35, shot to online fame after fleeing Britain while pregnant and documenting her 'dream life' in South-East Asia to thousands of followers. The mum-of-one, from Bamber Bridge, became a controversial figure on social media after openly sharing tips on how to claim benefits from abroad and bragging how her 'dream life' in Thailand was funded by fiddling £2,300 per month in British benefits. But her sunshine lifestyle soon came crashing down. Last March, Matthews was arrested by immigration officials at her home in Pattaya and locked up in what she described as a 'hell hole prison' crawling with rats and cockroaches. Her visa was cancelled and she spent 15 weeks behind bars in a Bangkok detention centre before being deported back to the UK - leaving her young son behind with family. Within weeks of returning to Lancashire last June, Matthews was arrested over drugs offences. She later admitted supplying cocaine, as well as possessing cocaine and cannabis.
  • 172 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Brit who boasted she claimed UK benefits while in Thailand is jailed

Do you really think that Labour support will improve with Starmer gone? Labour’s first two years have displayed weak economic growth, a slowing jobs market, and pressure on businesses from higher taxes and regulation. Public spending has risen while services remain strained. Energy policy is seen as costly, relying on backup generation, and high immigration continues to add pressure on housing and infrastructure. Overall, critics point to a growing state, limited growth, and a disconnect between government priorities and everyday concerns.

Baywatch cast brush off criticism and get back into their iconic red swimsuits to shoot beach scenes as show reboot is panned for its cast of 'influencers' with 'no acting ability'

Baywatch cast have brushed off criticism as they slipped back into their iconic red swimsuits to shoot dramatic beach scenes on Wednesday. Hours after the show reboot was panned for its cast of 'influencers' with 'no acting ability', model Brooks Nader flaunted her jaw-dropping figure in the red swimwear as she arrived at the beach wearing a pair of UGGs.  The Sports Illustrated star, 29, was joined on set by influencer-turned-actor Noah Beck, 24, who flaunted her chiselled abs as he ran along the beach shirtless in an intense scene.
  • 81 comments
  • 2 videos
  • share Baywatch cast get back into iconic red swimsuits ignoring criticism
